title: Some customer phone numbers are wrong in TaskFlow
body: We noticed that some of our customer contact records have incorrect phone numbers. The numbers look truncated or mangled. Our support team caught it because they tried calling a few customers and got wrong numbers. We have about 2,000 customer records and I'd estimate maybe 50-100 have wrong numbers. This is a serious data integrity issue for us.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — data integrity issues like this are definitely something we want to get to the bottom of quickly.

To help us narrow down what's happening: do you have a sense of **when** the incorrect phone numbers first appeared? For example, were these records correct at some point and then changed, or could they have been wrong since they were originally entered? And did this coincide with any recent changes — like a software update, a data import/migration, or a bulk edit of customer records?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I think they were correct before — we've been using these records for a while without issues. It started maybe 2 weeks ago? That's around when our support team first started noticing wrong numbers when trying to reach customers. We do have a nightly batch import that syncs customer data from our Salesforce CRM into TaskFlow, so it's possible something changed there. I'm not sure if there was a software update around that time, but the timing does line up with roughly two weeks ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful — the timing lining up with the nightly Salesforce sync is a strong lead.

Could you share a couple of examples of what the corrupted phone numbers look like compared to what they should be? For instance, if a correct number is `+1-555-867-5309`, what does the mangled version look like in TaskFlow — is it truncated (like `555-867-53`), missing a country code, reformatted differently, or something else? Even 2–3 before/after comparisons would help us pinpoint exactly where the data is getting altered.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah, I pulled up a few that our support team flagged. One customer is in Ireland and their number should be +353 1 234 5678 but in TaskFlow it shows as +35 1 234 5678 — so it looks like the country code got cut short. Another one is a customer in Antigua, their number starts with +1268 but in TaskFlow it's showing as +12 and then the rest of the number. Meanwhile our US and UK customers seem fine — their numbers with +1 and +44 look correct. I haven't done an exhaustive check but that's the pattern I've noticed so far from the ones support flagged.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That pattern is really telling — it looks like country codes longer than 2 digits are being clipped to exactly 2 characters, which would explain why `+353` becomes `+35` and `+1268` becomes `+12`, while `+1` and `+44` are untouched.

Two things that would help us zero in on the root cause:

1. **Can you check whether the affected records were all recently modified by the nightly Salesforce sync?** For example, do those records have a `last_modified` or `updated_at` timestamp that lines up with the batch job, versus records that were entered or edited manually in TaskFlow?

2. **Are you aware of any changes to the sync job or TaskFlow's database schema around two weeks ago?** Things like a new field mapping, a migration that altered the phone number column, or an update to the integration connector?

This would help us confirm whether the sync is the culprit versus something else silently modifying records.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body:
I checked a couple of the affected records and yeah, they all show updated timestamps that match our nightly sync schedule — looks like they were last touched during the batch job runs. The ones that are correct (like our US customers) also get synced nightly but their numbers are fine, so it's not like the sync is breaking everything.

As for changes — I'm not 100% sure, but I think one of our developers did some work on how phone numbers are handled, something about supporting international formats better. That was roughly around two weeks ago, which would line up. I'd have to check with the dev team to get the exact details of what they changed though. I also noticed the numbers in our actual Salesforce CRM are still correct, so whatever is happening seems to be on the TaskFlow side during the import.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Nightly Salesforce sync truncates phone country codes longer than 2 digits","problem":"Customer phone numbers with country codes longer than 2 digits (e.g., +353 Ireland, +1268 Antigua) are being truncated to 2 characters during the nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow batch import. Numbers with 1- or 2-digit country codes (+1 US, +44 UK) are unaffected. The source data in Salesforce remains correct; corruption occurs on the TaskFlow side during import.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A code change approximately two weeks ago — described as improving international phone number format support — likely introduced a parsing or formatting step that truncates the country code field to a maximum of 2 characters. This logic is applied during the nightly batch sync, so only synced records are affected.","reproduction_steps":["Identify the code change from ~2 weeks ago related to international phone number handling","Set up a test customer record in Salesforce with a country code longer than 2 digits (e.g., +353 1 234 5678 for Ireland)","Run the nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow sync (or invoke the sync function directly)","Verify the phone number in TaskFlow — expect the country code to be truncated to 2 characters (+35 1 234 5678)","Repeat with a 1-digit country code (+1) and confirm it is unaffected"],"environment":"TaskFlow production environment with nightly batch import from Salesforce CRM. Approximately 2,000 customer records synced. Issue began ~2 weeks ago coinciding with a developer change to phone number handling.","severity":"high","impact":"Approximately 50-100 customer records have corrupted phone numbers. Support team cannot reach affected international customers. All customers with country codes of 3+ digits are potentially affected on each sync run, meaning the corruption is ongoing and may worsen as more records are touched.","recommended_fix":"1. Locate the commit from ~2 weeks ago that modified phone number parsing/formatting in the sync pipeline. 2. Look for a hard-coded length limit, substring operation, or field width constraint on the country code portion (likely capping at 2 characters). 3. Fix the parsing to accommodate country codes of 1-3 digits per the E.164 standard. 4. Run a one-time remediation to re-sync affected records from Salesforce to restore correct phone numbers.","proposed_test_case":"Unit test the phone number parsing/formatting function with country codes of varying lengths: +1 (1 digit), +44 (2 digits), +353 (3 digits), +1268 (4-digit area code treated as country code). Assert that all digits are preserved through the transformation. Add an integration test that syncs a record with a 3-digit country code and verifies the stored value matches the source.","information_gaps":["Exact commit or PR that changed phone number handling ~2 weeks ago","Whether the truncation happens in a parsing function, a database column constraint, or a field mapping configuration","Full count of affected records (reporter estimated 50-100 but hasn't done exhaustive audit)"]}
